mystery.title: The Vermillion Hour
mystery.teaser: A celebrated pianist collapses at midnight behind locked windows and candlelight.

clues.clue: A half-burnt letter in violet ink threatens ruin if a debt is called in.
A sharp scent of bergamot lingers near the piano bench.
Klaus heard the sonata stop abruptly at 11:47, long before the alarm was raised.
Liesl's umbrella is soaked, but her overcoat is completely dry.
An empty laudanum vial marked with Dr. Roth's apothecary seal lies behind the drape.
The latched casement window has freshly oiled hinges.
A hidden ledger proves Margarethe is drowning in debt to the victim.

suspect.name: Klaus Wender
suspect.alibi: He claims he was reading by the lobby fire all evening.

suspects.suspect: Concierge on duty and first to witness the aftermath.

suspect.name: Margarethe Holst
suspect.alibi: She says she stayed in her suite dictating letters.

suspects.suspect: Patroness with strong social standing and hidden financial pressure.

suspect.name: Dr. Emil Roth
suspect.alibi: He says he was treating a child two doors down.

suspects.suspect: House physician whose medicine appears at the scene.

suspect.name: Liesl Berger
suspect.alibi: She says she left at eleven and walked home in the rain.

suspects.suspect: Young page turner who may have returned unseen.

scene.title: The Last Sonata
scene.narrative: At fifteen past midnight you enter Pension Adelgunde. Frau Anneliese Vogt lies over the piano keys, candles burning low, and the corridor guests stand in shocked silence.
scene.choice: Examine the body and piano first.

scene.title: The Bosendorfer Speaks
scene.narrative: No obvious wound appears, but violet lips suggest poison. A burnt letter is trapped between strings and bergamot hangs in the air.
scene.choice: Inspect the velvet drape and window.

scene.title: The Concierge's Account
scene.narrative: Klaus insists no one used the main stair after eleven, yet the piano stopped at 11:47. The timeline opens a twelve-minute void.
scene.choice: Search the music room in detail.

scene.title: The Cloakroom Contradiction
scene.narrative: Liesl's umbrella drips beside a perfectly dry coat. Her account of walking home in rain does not hold together.
scene.choice: Ignore her for now and inspect the drape.

scene.title: Behind the Velvet Drape
scene.narrative: In a hidden alcove you recover an empty laudanum vial. The casement is latched, but silent freshly oiled hinges reveal recent use.
scene.choice: Confront Dr. Roth about the vial.
Use the letter to pressure Margarethe.
Question Liesl about returning by the garden door.

scene.title: The Page Turner Falters
scene.narrative: Liesl admits she returned for her folio and heard two women arguing. She insists she touched nothing and fled in panic.
scene.choice: Confront Margarethe with the violet-ink threat.
Recheck the drape and window mechanism.

scene.title: A Closed Door
scene.narrative: Without hard evidence, Margarethe dismisses your questions and retreats behind locked doors. The trail cools before dawn.

scene.title: The Physician's Admission
scene.narrative: Roth confirms the vial is his but says it was full earlier in the victim's drawer. He quietly adds that Margarethe dined privately with the pianist.
scene.choice: Confront Margarethe with letter and poison trail.
Search her suite for financial motive.

scene.title: The Ledger in the Drawer
scene.narrative: A hidden ledger confirms catastrophic debt and promissory notes owed to Anneliese. Ruin by morning was imminent.
scene.choice: Present the full chain of motive and method.

scene.title: The Vermillion Hour
scene.narrative: Faced with the letter, scent, laudanum trail, and oiled window route, Margarethe confesses she chose the hour before bankruptcy chose her fate.
